verses.latin_vulgate: In tempore illo, dicit Dominus,  ero Deus universis cognationibus Israël,  et ipsi erunt mihi in populum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּעֵ֤ת הַהִיא֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה אֶֽהְיֶה֙ לֵֽאלֹהִ֔ים לְכֹ֖ל מִשְׁפְּח֣וֹת יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וְהֵ֖מָּה יִֽהְיוּ־לִ֥י לְעָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: At the same time, saith the Lord, will I be the God of all the families of Israel, and they shall be my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Invenit gratiam in deserto populus qui remanserat a gladio :  vadet ad requiem suam Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה מָצָ֥א חֵן֙ בַּמִּדְבָּ֔ר עַ֖ם שְׂרִ֣ידֵי חָ֑רֶב הָל֥וֹךְ לְהַרְגִּיע֖וֹ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, The people which were left of the sword found grace in the wilderness; even Israel, when I went to cause him to rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Longe Dominus apparuit mihi.  Et in caritate perpetua dilexi te :  ideo attraxi te, miserans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵרָח֕וֹק יְהֹוָ֖ה נִרְאָ֣ה לִ֑י וְאַהֲבַ֤ת עוֹלָם֙ אֲהַבְתִּ֔יךְ עַל־כֵּ֖ן מְשַׁכְתִּ֥יךְ חָֽסֶד׃
verses.king_james: The Lord hath appeared of old unto me, saying, Yea, I have loved thee with an everlasting love: therefore with lovingkindness have I drawn thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursumque ædificabo te, et ædificaberis, virgo Israël :  adhuc ornaberis tympanis tuis,  et egredieris in choro ludentium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ע֤וֹד אֶבְנֵךְ֙ וְֽנִבְנֵ֔ית בְּתוּלַ֖ת יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל ע֚וֹד תַּעְדִּ֣י תֻפַּ֔יִךְ וְיָצָ֖את בִּמְח֥וֹל מְשַׂחֲקִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Again I will build thee, and thou shalt be built, O virgin of Israel: thou shalt again be adorned with thy tabrets, and shalt go forth in the dances of them that make merry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc plantabis vineas in montibus Samariæ :  plantabunt plantantes,  et donec tempus veniat, non vindemiabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ע֚וֹד תִּטְּעִ֣י כְרָמִ֔ים בְּהָרֵ֖י שֹֽׁמְר֑וֹן נָטְע֥וּ נֹטְעִ֖ים וְחִלֵּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: Thou shalt yet plant vines upon the mountains of Samaria: the planters shall plant, and shall eat them as common things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia erit dies in qua clamabunt custodes in monte Ephraim :  Surgite, et ascendamus in Sion ad Dominum Deum nostrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י יֶשׁ־י֔וֹם קָרְא֥וּ נֹצְרִ֖ים בְּהַ֣ר אֶפְרָ֑יִם ק֚וּמוּ וְנַעֲלֶ֣ה צִיּ֔וֹן אֶל־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: For there shall be a day, that the watchmen upon the mount Ephraim shall cry, Arise ye, and let us go up to Zion unto the Lord our God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia hæc dicit Dominus :  Exsultate in lætitia, Jacob,  et hinnite contra caput gentium :  personate, et canite, et dicite :  Salva, Domine, populum tuum, reliquias Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־כֹ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה רׇנּ֤וּ לְיַֽעֲקֹב֙ שִׂמְחָ֔ה וְצַהֲל֖וּ בְּרֹ֣אשׁ הַגּוֹיִ֑ם הַשְׁמִ֤יעוּ הַֽלְלוּ֙ וְאִמְר֔וּ הוֹשַׁ֤ע יְהֹוָה֙ אֶֽת־עַמְּךָ֔ אֵ֖ת שְׁאֵרִ֥ית יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: For thus saith the Lord; Sing with gladness for Jacob, and shout among the chief of the nations: publish ye, praise ye, and say, O Lord, save thy people, the remnant of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego adducam eos de terra aquilonis,  et congregabo eos ab extremis terræ :  inter quos erunt cæcus et claudus,  prægnans et pariens simul,  cœtus magnus revertentium huc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְנִי֩ מֵבִ֨יא אוֹתָ֜ם מֵאֶ֣רֶץ צָפ֗וֹן וְקִבַּצְתִּים֮ מִיַּרְכְּתֵי־אָ֒רֶץ֒ בָּ֚ם עִוֵּ֣ר וּפִסֵּ֔חַ הָרָ֥ה וְיֹלֶ֖דֶת יַחְדָּ֑ו קָהָ֥ל גָּד֖וֹל יָשׁ֥וּבוּ הֵֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I will bring them from the north country, and gather them from the coasts of the earth, and with them the blind and the lame, the woman with child and her that travaileth with child together: a great company shall return thither.

verses.latin_vulgate: In fletu venient,  et in misericordia reducam eos :  et adducam eos per torrentes aquarum in via recta,  et non impingent in ea,  quia factus sum Israëli pater,  et Ephraim primogenitus meus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּבְכִ֣י יָבֹ֗אוּ וּֽבְתַחֲנוּנִים֮ אֽוֹבִילֵם֒ אֽוֹלִיכֵם֙ אֶל־נַ֣חֲלֵי מַ֔יִם בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ יָשָׁ֔ר לֹ֥א יִכָּשְׁל֖וּ בָּ֑הּ כִּֽי־הָיִ֤יתִי לְיִשְׂרָאֵל֙ לְאָ֔ב וְאֶפְרַ֖יִם בְּכֹ֥רִי הֽוּא׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: They shall come with weeping, and with supplications will I lead them: I will cause them to walk by the rivers of waters in a straight way, wherein they shall not stumble: for I am a father to Israel, and Ephraim is my firstborn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite verbum Domini, gentes,  et annuntiate in insulis quæ procul sunt,  et dicite : Qui dispersit Israël congregabit eum,  et custodiet eum sicut pastor gregem suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֤וּ דְבַר־יְהֹוָה֙ גּוֹיִ֔ם וְהַגִּ֥ידוּ בָאִיִּ֖ים מִמֶּרְחָ֑ק וְאִמְר֗וּ מְזָרֵ֤ה יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ יְקַבְּצֶ֔נּוּ וּשְׁמָר֖וֹ כְּרֹעֶ֥ה עֶדְרֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Hear the word of the Lord, O ye nations, and declare it in the isles afar off, and say, He that scattered Israel will gather him, and keep him, as a shepherd doth his flock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Redemit enim Dominus Jacob,  et liberavit eum de manu potentioris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־פָדָ֥ה יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶֽת־יַעֲקֹ֑ב וּגְאָל֕וֹ מִיַּ֖ד חָזָ֥ק מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: For the Lord hath redeemed Jacob, and ransomed him from the hand of him that was stronger than he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venient, et laudabunt in monte Sion :  et confluent ad bona Domini,  super frumento, et vino, et oleo,  et fœtu pecorum et armentorum :  eritque anima eorum quasi hortus irriguus,  et ultra non esurient.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָ֘אוּ֮ וְרִנְּנ֣וּ בִמְרוֹם־צִיּוֹן֒ וְנָהֲר֞וּ אֶל־ט֣וּב יְהֹוָ֗ה עַל־דָּגָן֙ וְעַל־תִּירֹ֣שׁ וְעַל־יִצְהָ֔ר וְעַל־בְּנֵי־צֹ֖אן וּבָקָ֑ר וְהָיְתָ֤ה נַפְשָׁם֙ כְּגַ֣ן רָוֶ֔ה וְלֹא־יוֹסִ֥יפוּ לְדַאֲבָ֖ה עֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: Therefore they shall come and sing in the height of Zion, and shall flow together to the goodness of the Lord, for wheat, and for wine, and for oil, and for the young of the flock and of the herd: and their soul shall be as a watered garden; and they shall not sorrow any more at all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc lætabitur virgo in choro,  juvenes et senes simul :  et convertam luctum eorum in gaudium,  et consolabor eos, et lætificabo a dolore suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֣ז תִּשְׂמַ֤ח בְּתוּלָה֙ בְּמָח֔וֹל וּבַחֻרִ֥ים וּזְקֵנִ֖ים יַחְדָּ֑ו וְהָפַכְתִּ֨י אֶבְלָ֤ם לְשָׂשׂוֹן֙ וְנִ֣חַמְתִּ֔ים וְשִׂמַּחְתִּ֖ים מִיגוֹנָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Then shall the virgin rejoice in the dance, both young men and old together: for I will turn their mourning into joy, and will comfort them, and make them rejoice from their sorrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inebriabo animam sacerdotum pinguedine,  et populus meus bonis meis adimplebitur, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרִוֵּיתִ֛י נֶ֥פֶשׁ הַכֹּהֲנִ֖ים דָּ֑שֶׁן וְעַמִּ֛י אֶת־טוּבִ֥י יִשְׂבָּ֖עוּ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And I will satiate the soul of the priests with fatness, and my people shall be satisfied with my goodness, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Vox in excelso audita est lamentationis :  luctus, et fletus Rachel plorantis filios suos,  et nolentis consolari super eis,  quia non sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה ק֣וֹל בְּרָמָ֤ה נִשְׁמָע֙ נְהִי֙ בְּכִ֣י תַמְרוּרִ֔ים רָחֵ֖ל מְבַכָּ֣ה עַל־בָּנֶ֑יהָ מֵאֲנָ֛ה לְהִנָּחֵ֥ם עַל־בָּנֶ֖יהָ כִּ֥י אֵינֶֽנּוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord; A voice was heard in Ramah, lamentation, and bitter weeping; Rahel weeping for her children refused to be comforted for her children, because they were not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Quiescat vox tua a ploratu,  et oculi tui a lacrimis,  quia est merces operi tuo, ait Dominus,  et revertentur de terra inimici :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה מִנְעִ֤י קוֹלֵךְ֙ מִבֶּ֔כִי וְעֵינַ֖יִךְ מִדִּמְעָ֑ה כִּי֩ יֵ֨שׁ שָׂכָ֤ר לִפְעֻלָּתֵךְ֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה וְשָׁ֖בוּ מֵאֶ֥רֶץ אוֹיֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord; Refrain thy voice from weeping, and thine eyes from tears: for thy work shall be rewarded, saith the Lord; and they shall come again from the land of the enemy.

verses.latin_vulgate: et est spes novissimis tuis, ait Dominus,  et revertentur filii ad terminos suos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֵשׁ־תִּקְוָ֥ה לְאַחֲרִיתֵ֖ךְ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְשָׁ֥בוּ בָנִ֖ים לִגְבוּלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And there is hope in thine end, saith the Lord, that thy children shall come again to their own border.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiens audivi Ephraim transmigrantem :  Castigasti me, et eruditus sum,  quasi juvenculus indomitus :  converte me, et convertar,  quia tu Dominus Deus meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁמ֣וֹעַ שָׁמַ֗עְתִּי אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ מִתְנוֹדֵ֔ד יִסַּרְתַּ֙נִי֙ וָֽאִוָּסֵ֔ר כְּעֵ֖גֶל לֹ֣א לֻמָּ֑ד הֲשִׁבֵ֣נִי וְאָשׁ֔וּבָה כִּ֥י אַתָּ֖ה יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהָֽי׃
verses.king_james: I have surely heard Ephraim bemoaning himself thus; Thou hast chastised me, and I was chastised, as a bullock unaccustomed to the yoke: turn thou me, and I shall be turned; for thou art the Lord my God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam enim convertisti me, egi pœnitentiam :  et postquam ostendisti mihi, percussi femur meum.  Confusus sum, et erubui,  quoniam sustinui opprobrium adolescentiæ meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אַחֲרֵ֤י שׁוּבִי֙ נִחַ֔מְתִּי וְאַֽחֲרֵי֙ הִוָּ֣דְעִ֔י סָפַ֖קְתִּי עַל־יָרֵ֑ךְ בֹּ֚שְׁתִּי וְגַם־נִכְלַ֔מְתִּי כִּ֥י נָשָׂ֖אתִי חֶרְפַּ֥ת נְעוּרָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Surely after that I was turned, I repented; and after that I was instructed, I smote upon my thigh: I was ashamed, yea, even confounded, because I did bear the reproach of my youth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si filius honorabilis mihi Ephraim,  si puer delicatus !  quia ex quo locutus sum de eo,  adhuc recordabor ejus.  Idcirco conturbata sunt viscera mea super eum :  miserans miserebor ejus, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲבֵן֩ יַקִּ֨יר לִ֜י אֶפְרַ֗יִם אִ֚ם יֶ֣לֶד שַׁעֲשֻׁעִ֔ים כִּֽי־מִדֵּ֤י דַבְּרִי֙ בּ֔וֹ זָכֹ֥ר אֶזְכְּרֶ֖נּוּ ע֑וֹד עַל־כֵּ֗ן הָמ֤וּ מֵעַי֙ ל֔וֹ רַחֵ֥ם אֲֽרַחֲמֶ֖נּוּ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Is Ephraim my dear son? is he a pleasant child? for since I spake against him, I do earnestly remember him still: therefore my bowels are troubled for him; I will surely have mercy upon him, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Statue tibi speculam ;  pone tibi amaritudines ;  dirige cor tuum in viam rectam in qua ambulasti :  revertere, virgo Israël,  revertere ad civitates tuas istas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַצִּ֧יבִי לָ֣ךְ צִיֻּנִ֗ים שִׂ֤מִי לָךְ֙ תַּמְרוּרִ֔ים שִׁ֣תִי לִבֵּ֔ךְ לַֽמְסִלָּ֖ה דֶּ֣רֶךְ (הלכתי) [הָלָ֑כְתְּ] שׁ֚וּבִי בְּתוּלַ֣ת יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל שֻׁ֖בִי אֶל־עָרַ֥יִךְ אֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.king_james: Set thee up waymarks, make thee high heaps: set thine heart toward the highway, even the way which thou wentest: turn again, O virgin of Israel, turn again to these thy cities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Usquequo deliciis dissolveris, filia vaga ?  quia creavit Dominus novum super terram :  femina circumdabit virum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַד־מָתַי֙ תִּתְחַמָּקִ֔ין הַבַּ֖ת הַשּֽׁוֹבֵבָ֑ה כִּֽי־בָרָ֨א יְהֹוָ֤ה חֲדָשָׁה֙ בָּאָ֔רֶץ נְקֵבָ֖ה תְּס֥וֹבֵֽב גָּֽבֶר׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: How long wilt thou go about, O thou backsliding daughter? for the Lord hath created a new thing in the earth, A woman shall compass a man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël :  Adhuc dicent verbum istud  in terra Juda et in urbibus ejus,  cum convertero captivitatem eorum :  Benedicat tibi Dominus, pulchritudo justitiæ, mons sanctus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּֽה־אָמַ֞ר יְהֹוָ֤ה צְבָאוֹת֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל ע֣וֹד יֹאמְר֞וּ אֶת־הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֗ה בְּאֶ֤רֶץ יְהוּדָה֙ וּבְעָרָ֔יו בְּשׁוּבִ֖י אֶת־שְׁבוּתָ֑ם יְבָרֶכְךָ֧ יְהֹוָ֛ה נְוֵה־צֶ֖דֶק הַ֥ר הַקֹּֽדֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel; As yet they shall use this speech in the land of Judah and in the cities thereof, when I shall bring again their captivity; The Lord bless thee, O habitation of justice, and mountain of holiness.

verses.latin_vulgate: et habitabunt in eo Judas et omnes civitates ejus simul,  agricolæ et minantes greges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָ֥שְׁבוּ בָ֛הּ יְהוּדָ֥ה וְכׇל־עָרָ֖יו יַחְדָּ֑ו אִכָּרִ֕ים וְנָסְע֖וּ בַּעֵֽדֶר׃
verses.king_james: And there shall dwell in Judah itself, and in all the cities thereof together, husbandmen, and they that go forth with flocks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia inebriavi animam lassam,  et omnem animam esurientem saturavi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֥י הִרְוֵ֖יתִי נֶ֣פֶשׁ עֲיֵפָ֑ה וְכׇל־נֶ֥פֶשׁ דָּאֲבָ֖ה מִלֵּֽאתִי׃
verses.king_james: For I have satiated the weary soul, and I have replenished every sorrowful soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo quasi de somno suscitatus sum :  et vidi, et somnus meus dulcis mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־זֹ֖את הֱקִיצֹ֣תִי וָאֶרְאֶ֑ה וּשְׁנָתִ֖י עָ֥רְבָה לִּֽי׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Upon this I awaked, and beheld; and my sleep was sweet unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus,  et seminabo domum Israël et domum Juda  semine hominum et semine jumentorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֛ה יָמִ֥ים בָּאִ֖ים נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְזָרַעְתִּ֗י אֶת־בֵּ֤ית יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ וְאֶת־בֵּ֣ית יְהוּדָ֔ה זֶ֥רַע אָדָ֖ם וְזֶ֥רַע בְּהֵמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will sow the house of Israel and the house of Judah with the seed of man, and with the seed of beast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sicut vigilavi super eos  ut evellerem, et demolirer, et dissiparem,  et disperderem, et affligerem,  sic vigilabo super eos  ut ædificem et plantem, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֞ה כַּאֲשֶׁ֧ר שָׁקַ֣דְתִּי עֲלֵיהֶ֗ם לִנְת֧וֹשׁ וְלִנְת֛וֹץ וְלַהֲרֹ֖ס וּלְהַאֲבִ֣יד וּלְהָרֵ֑עַ כֵּ֣ן אֶשְׁקֹ֧ד עֲלֵיהֶ֛ם לִבְנ֥וֹת וְלִנְטֹ֖עַ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass, that like as I have watched over them, to pluck up, and to break down, and to throw down, and to destroy, and to afflict; so will I watch over them, to build, and to plant, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus illis non dicent ultra :  Patres comederunt uvam acerbam,  et dentes filiorum obstupuerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיָּמִ֣ים הָהֵ֔ם לֹא־יֹאמְר֣וּ ע֔וֹד אָב֖וֹת אָ֣כְלוּ בֹ֑סֶר וְשִׁנֵּ֥י בָנִ֖ים תִּקְהֶֽינָה׃
verses.king_james: In those days they shall say no more, The fathers have eaten a sour grape, and the children’s teeth are set on edge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed unusquisque in iniquitate sua morietur :  omnis homo qui comederit uvam acerbam,  obstupescent dentes ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י אִם־אִ֥ישׁ בַּעֲוֺנ֖וֹ יָמ֑וּת כׇּל־הָֽאָדָ֛ם הָאֹכֵ֥ל הַבֹּ֖סֶר תִּקְהֶ֥ינָה שִׁנָּֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: But every one shall die for his own iniquity: every man that eateth the sour grape, his teeth shall be set on edge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce dies venient, dicit Dominus,  et feriam domui Israël et domui Juda fœdus novum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֛ה יָמִ֥ים בָּאִ֖ים נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְכָרַתִּ֗י אֶת־בֵּ֧ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל וְאֶת־בֵּ֥ית יְהוּדָ֖ה בְּרִ֥ית חֲדָשָֽׁה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel, and with the house of Judah:

verses.latin_vulgate: non secundum pactum quod pepigi cum patribus eorum  in die qua apprehendi manum eorum  ut educerem eos de terra Ægypti,  pactum quod irritum fecerunt :  et ego dominatus sum eorum, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֣א כַבְּרִ֗ית אֲשֶׁ֤ר כָּרַ֙תִּי֙ אֶת־אֲבוֹתָ֔ם בְּיוֹם֙ הֶחֱזִיקִ֣י בְיָדָ֔ם לְהוֹצִיאָ֖ם מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרָ֑יִם אֲשֶׁר־הֵ֜מָּה הֵפֵ֣רוּ אֶת־בְּרִיתִ֗י וְאָנֹכִ֛י בָּעַ֥לְתִּי בָ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day that I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt; which my covenant they brake, although I was an husband unto them, saith the Lord:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed hoc erit pactum quod feriam cum domo Israël  post dies illos, dicit Dominus :  dabo legem meam in visceribus eorum,  et in corde eorum scribam eam,  et ero eis in Deum,  et ipsi erunt mihi in populum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י זֹ֣את הַבְּרִ֡ית אֲשֶׁ֣ר אֶכְרֹת֩ אֶת־בֵּ֨ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל אַחֲרֵ֨י הַיָּמִ֤ים הָהֵם֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה נָתַ֤תִּי אֶת־תּֽוֹרָתִי֙ בְּקִרְבָּ֔ם וְעַל־לִבָּ֖ם אֶכְתְּבֶ֑נָּה וְהָיִ֤יתִי לָהֶם֙ לֵֽאלֹהִ֔ים וְהֵ֖מָּה יִֽהְיוּ־לִ֥י לְעָֽם׃
verses.king_james: But this shall be the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel; After those days, saith the Lord, I will put my law in their inward parts, and write it in their hearts; and will be their God, and they shall be my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: et non docebit ultra vir proximum suum  et vir fratrem suum,  dicens : Cognosce Dominum :  omnes enim cognoscent me,  a minimo eorum usque ad maximum, ait Dominus :  quia propitiabor iniquitati eorum,  et peccati eorum non memorabor amplius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֧א יְלַמְּד֣וּ ע֗וֹד אִ֣ישׁ אֶת־רֵעֵ֜הוּ וְאִ֤ישׁ אֶת־אָחִיו֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר דְּע֖וּ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֑ה כִּֽי־כוּלָּם֩ יֵדְע֨וּ אוֹתִ֜י לְמִקְּטַנָּ֤ם וְעַד־גְּדוֹלָם֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֤י אֶסְלַח֙ לַֽעֲוֺנָ֔ם וּלְחַטָּאתָ֖ם לֹ֥א אֶזְכָּר־עֽוֹד׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And they shall teach no more every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the Lord: for they shall all know me, from the least of them unto the greatest of them, saith the Lord: for I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus qui dat solem in lumine diei,  ordinem lunæ et stellarum in lumine noctis :  qui turbat mare, et sonant fluctus ejus :  Dominus exercituum nomen illi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה נֹתֵ֥ן שֶׁ֙מֶשׁ֙ לְא֣וֹר יוֹמָ֔ם חֻקֹּ֛ת יָרֵ֥חַ וְכוֹכָבִ֖ים לְא֣וֹר לָ֑יְלָה רֹגַ֤ע הַיָּם֙ וַיֶּהֱמ֣וּ גַלָּ֔יו יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, which giveth the sun for a light by day, and the ordinances of the moon and of the stars for a light by night, which divideth the sea when the waves thereof roar; The Lord of hosts is his name:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si defecerint leges istæ coram me, dicit Dominus,  tunc et semen Israël deficiet,  ut non sit gens coram me cunctis diebus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יָמֻ֜שׁוּ הַחֻקִּ֥ים הָאֵ֛לֶּה מִלְּפָנַ֖י נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה גַּם֩ זֶ֨רַע יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל יִשְׁבְּת֗וּ מִהְי֥וֹת גּ֛וֹי לְפָנַ֖י כׇּל־הַיָּמִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: If those ordinances depart from before me, saith the Lord, then the seed of Israel also shall cease from being a nation before me for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Si mensurari potuerint cæli sursum,  et investigari fundamenta terræ deorsum,  et ego abjiciam universum semen Israël,  propter omnia quæ fecerunt, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה אִם־יִמַּ֤דּוּ שָׁמַ֙יִם֙ מִלְמַ֔עְלָה וְיֵחָקְר֥וּ מוֹסְדֵי־אֶ֖רֶץ לְמָ֑טָּה גַּם־אֲנִ֞י אֶמְאַ֨ס בְּכׇל־זֶ֧רַע יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל עַֽל־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשׂ֖וּ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord; If heaven above can be measured, and the foundations of the earth searched out beneath, I will also cast off all the seed of Israel for all that they have done, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus,  et ædificabitur civitas Domino,  a turre Hananeel usque ad portam anguli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֛ה יָמִ֥ים [בָּאִ֖ים] נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְנִבְנְתָ֤ה הָעִיר֙ לַיהֹוָ֔ה מִמִּגְדַּ֥ל חֲנַנְאֵ֖ל שַׁ֥עַר הַפִּנָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that the city shall be built to the Lord from the tower of Hananeel unto the gate of the corner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exibit ultra norma mensuræ  in conspectu ejus super collem Gareb,  et circuibit Goatha,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָצָ֨א ע֜וֹד (קוה) [קָ֤ו] הַמִּדָּה֙ נֶגְדּ֔וֹ עַ֖ל גִּבְעַ֣ת גָּרֵ֑ב וְנָסַ֖ב גֹּעָֽתָה׃
verses.king_james: And the measuring line shall yet go forth over against it upon the hill Gareb, and shall compass about to Goath.

verses.latin_vulgate: et omnem vallem cadaverum, et cineris,  et universam regionem mortis  usque ad torrentem Cedron,  et usque ad angulum portæ equorum orientalis,  Sanctum Domini :  non evelletur, et non destruetur ultra in perpetuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכׇל־הָעֵ֣מֶק הַפְּגָרִ֣ים ׀ וְהַדֶּ֡שֶׁן וְכׇֽל־[הַשְּׁדֵמוֹת֩] (השרמות) עַד־נַ֨חַל קִדְר֜וֹן עַד־פִּנַּ֨ת שַׁ֤עַר הַסּוּסִים֙ מִזְרָ֔חָה קֹ֖דֶשׁ לַיהֹוָ֑ה לֹא־יִנָּתֵ֧שׁ וְֽלֹא־יֵהָרֵ֛ס ע֖וֹד לְעוֹלָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And the whole valley of the dead bodies, and of the ashes, and all the fields unto the brook of Kidron, unto the corner of the horse gate toward the east, shall be holy unto the Lord; it shall not be plucked up, nor thrown down any more for ever.
